overview: Root cause: `hide_qt_warning` and `QtWarningFilter` were relocated from `qutebrowser/utils/log.py` to `qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py`, but no test file `test_qtlog.py` was ever created to cover the relocated components. The implementation (qtlog.py:34-66) and backward-compatible re-exports (log.py:34-36) are fully correct. The only fix needed is to create a new test file `tests/unit/utils/test_qtlog.py` that imports directly from the `qtlog` module and exercises the context manager and filter class per the behavioral requirements. This mirrors the established precedent from `test_log.py`'s `test_py_warning_filter` and `TestLogFilter` test patterns.

edits.change_rationale: exact_code_region: qtlog.py:34-48 (hide_qt_warning), qtlog.py:51-66 (QtWarningFilter), log.py:34-36 (backward-compatible re-exports). constraint: 'similar_implementation_patterns' — log.py py_warning_filter context manager (log.py:227-237) has dedicated tests test_py_warning_filter and test_py_warning_filter_error in test_log.py, establishing the precedent for how hide_qt_warning should be tested. constraint: 'missing_elements_to_implement' — both hide_qt_warning and QtWarningFilter are listed as missing test coverage. co_edit_relation: test imports from qtlog must not break the log.py re-export chain (qtlog -> log.py:34-36 -> qtnetworkdownloads.py:124). regression_expectations require that filtering behavior (strip-before-startswith, prefix suppression, multi-logger consistency) be verified by automated tests.
preserved_findings: The QtWarningFilter.filter() method (qtlog.py:63-66) returns True (allow log) when the record message does NOT start with the pattern
The filter checks `record.msg.strip().startswith(self._pattern)` and returns `False` when it matches (suppresses)
any(msg.strip().startswith(pattern) for pattern in suppressed_msgs) — prefix-check pattern confirms startswith is the intended matching semantics
qt_message_handler at qtlog.py:203 uses identical msg.strip().startswith(pattern) for suppressed_msgs prefix-check
The context manager should maintain consistent suppression behavior when applied to different Qt logger instances
same QtWarningFilter instance added to every logger via addFilter
The strip()-before-startswith pattern is consistent throughout the module

Filter pattern matching should properly handle warning messages that include leading whitespace characters or trailing spaces by applying the pattern comparison logic against the trimmed message content
